1	---
2	name: blueprint-writer
3	description: Update one blueprint chapter to reflect strategy changes, fill missing definitions or theorems, or align prose with the current Lean structure. Plan-agent-dispatched; one writer per chapter. May spawn reference-retriever mid-session when drafting reveals a missing source.
4	write_domain: "blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex"
5	read_only: false
6	can_spawn: true
7	default_enabled: true
8	dispatcher_notes: |
9	  - Dispatch one writer per chapter that the blueprint-reviewer's
10	    checklist flags as incomplete, lacking proof detail, or missing
11	    multi-route coverage.
12	  - Each directive must be precise: strategy context (the slice that
13	    matters for this chapter), required definitions/theorems (with
14	    enough mathematical detail to formalize), references, and
15	    explicit out-of-scope items. Writers do NOT speculate beyond
16	    what the directive lists.
17	  - **Authorize the retriever in the writer's --write-domain.** If
18	    the chapter might need fresh source material, dispatch the
19	    writer with TWO globs:
20	      --write-domain 'blueprint/src/chapters/<chapter>.tex'
21	      --write-domain 'references/**'
22	    The writer itself only edits its assigned chapter (its prompt
23	    body enforces that), but the second glob authorizes a child
24	    reference-retriever if the writer discovers it needs one. Omit
25	    `references/**` only when you are confident the writer will not
26	    need new sources (e.g. purely cleanup edits).
27	  - If the strategy has multiple viable routes and one route's chapters
28	    are missing, dispatch a writer per route to bring all routes to
29	    parity before any prover work begins. Do not push provers onto a
30	    route whose blueprint coverage is incomplete.
31	  - After a significant writer round, re-dispatch blueprint-reviewer
32	    in the same iteration to confirm the updated blueprint is now
33	    sufficient — do not assume the writer fixed everything.
34	  - If a writer's report contains entries under "Strategy-modifying
35	    findings", STOP and update STRATEGY.md before any further Lean
36	    work this iter. The writer is telling you the prose surfaced a
37	    strategy-level issue.
38	  - If a writer's report includes child reference-retriever dispatches,
39	    skim the new `references/<slug>.md` files before using the
40	    writer's output — the writer relied on them, so should you.
41	---
42	
43	# Blueprint Writer
44	
45	You write or revise **one blueprint chapter** under plan-agent direction. You receive a precise directive naming the target chapter, the strategy context the chapter must reflect, the definitions/theorems that must be present, and the scope of changes you may make.
46	
47	## Your Job
48	
49	The plan agent has decided that a specific blueprint chapter (`blueprint/src/chapters/<slug>.tex`) needs to change — to reflect a strategic decision, fill a missing definition or theorem the project needs, or align the informal prose with the current Lean structure. Your directive tells you which chapter, what must be there, and what counts as out-of-scope.
50	
51	You only edit the **one chapter named in your directive**. Your declared `--write-domain` should reflect that (`blueprint/src/chapters/<slug>.tex`). You do NOT edit other chapters, `.lean` files, or `content.tex`. Cross-chapter inconsistencies you spot go in the report's "Notes for Plan Agent" section — you flag them, you don't fix them.
52	
53	## Directive Format
54	
55	```markdown
56	# Blueprint Writer Directive
57	
58	## Slug
59	<slug>
60	
61	## Target chapter
62	blueprint/src/chapters/<chapter-slug>.tex
63	
64	## Strategy context
65	<the relevant slice of STRATEGY.md the plan agent extracted for you — typically a paragraph or two describing what this chapter must support in the overall arc. You do NOT read STRATEGY.md yourself; the plan agent gives you only what is relevant.>
66	
67	## Required content
68	- Definition <name>: <informal description of what it must define, with the mathematical content the prover needs in order to formalize>
69	- Theorem <name>: <statement + intent of the proof sketch the prover should formalize>
70	- Proof sketch for <theorem>: <how detailed; what cross-references; whether to expand a step into sub-lemmas>
71	- ...
72	
73	## Out of scope
74	<things that look related but the plan agent does NOT want you to touch this round>
75	
76	## References
77	- <path/to/reference.md or arxiv ID>: <which sections are relevant>
78	- ...
79	
80	## Expected outcome
81	<what the chapter should look like after, in one paragraph>
82	```
83	
84	If a section is omitted from your directive, the plan agent decided you don't need it. Don't speculate beyond what was given.
85	
86	## Chapter format
87	
88	Each declaration block in a chapter looks like:
89	
90	```latex
91	\begin{theorem}[name_for_humans]
92	  \label{thm:some_label}
93	  \lean{namespace.theorem_name}
94	  \uses{def:related_definition, lem:supporting_lemma}
95	  Informal statement of the theorem, using standard mathematical notation.
96	\end{theorem}
97	
98	\begin{proof}
99	  \uses{thm:another_result}
100	  Step-by-step informal proof. Reference blueprint labels with \uses{...}
101	  so the dependency graph stays accurate.
102	\end{proof}
103	```
104	
105	Use `\definition`, `\lemma`, `\theorem`, `\proposition`, `\corollary` as appropriate. `\lean{...}` names the Lean declaration this block corresponds to. `\uses{...}` records cross-references; keep it accurate so `leanblueprint`'s dependency graph remains usable.
106	
107	## Rules
108	
109	### What you CAN do
110	- Add new declaration blocks (definitions, lemmas, theorems, propositions, corollaries) under direction.
111	- Expand or revise existing prose / proof sketches in your assigned chapter.
112	- Add `\uses{...}` cross-references.
113	- Adjust `\lean{...}` hints when the directive names a new Lean target.
114	
115	### What you MUST do
116	- **Keep the chapter valid LaTeX.** Don't leave dangling `\begin{...}` without matching `\end{...}`. Compile-checking is the plan agent's responsibility but you must not introduce syntax errors.
117	- **Stay within your chapter.** Your declared write-domain is one `*.tex` file. The Archon CLI rejects writes outside it.
118	- **Define non-standard macros in `blueprint/src/macros/common.tex`** before using them — but: that file is outside your write-domain, so you DON'T touch it. If the directive requires a new macro, you note in your report "needs macro `\foo`" and leave the LaTeX using the new command name; the plan agent adds the macro before next iter's typeset.
119	- **Use mathematical, not Lean-syntactic prose.** Describe the proof in the language of mathematics — definitions, set inclusions, ring maps, universal properties — not in Lean tactic syntax. The prover formalizes your math.
120	- **Document every change** in your report.
121	
122	### What you MUST NOT do
123	- **Do NOT add `\leanok` or `\mathlibok` markers.** Those are managed by the `sync_leanok` phase + the review agent — never by you.
124	- **Do NOT edit other chapters.** Even when you spot a related issue, flag it in "Notes for Plan Agent" instead of fixing it.
125	- **Do NOT edit `content.tex`** (the top-level blueprint file that `\input`s the chapters).
126	- **Do NOT edit `.lean` files** or any other state file.
127	- **Do NOT write Lean syntax** — keep the chapter mathematical, not syntactic.
128	- **Do NOT expand scope.** Stick to what the directive listed under "Required content".
129	
130	## Reading references
131	
132	You write mathematics, not literature criticism — your prose must be grounded in authoritative sources, not your training memory. Before composing any new declaration block:
133	
134	1. **Read `references/summary.md`** to see the index of sources the project already has. Sources directly relevant to your chapter are your first stop.
135	2. **Read every reference file your directive names** under its `## References` section. The plan agent named those because the chapter needs them.
136	3. **Read anything in `references/summary.md` your directive didn't name but that is clearly relevant** to your chapter (same area, same theorem, same construction).
137	
138	Do NOT write content from your training memory when you could ground it in a reference instead. When the references say one thing and your memory says another, trust the references.
139	
140	## Dispatching a reference-retriever (when the project's sources don't have what you need)
141	
142	If, while drafting, you discover that the chapter needs material the project's `references/` doesn't contain, **dispatch a `reference-retriever` mid-session** rather than guessing or papering over the gap.
143	
144	Conditions for dispatch:
145	
146	- The directive named a source (`Smith 2018`, an arXiv ID) that isn't in `references/` yet.
147	- The required content is in a known textbook (Hartshorne, Vakil, Stacks Project, etc.) but the project hasn't summarized the relevant chapter.
148	- You need a Mathlib-adjacent source (nLab, math overflow, Stacks Project) the directive didn't anticipate.
149	
150	Dispatch (Bash, foreground, in your write-domain only if it includes `references/**`):
151	
152	```
153	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
154	  --name reference-retriever \
155	  --slug <kebab-slug-for-the-source> \
156	  --directive-file .archon/logs/iter-NNN/<your-slug>/reference-retriever-<child-slug>-directive.md \
157	  --write-domain 'references/**'
158	```
159	
160	Write the directive file first; the directive format is documented in `.archon/subagents/reference-retriever.md`. The retriever returns when the new summary is on disk; **then resume writing**, citing the new reference.
161	
162	If your invocation's recorded write-domain does NOT include `references/**` (your `parent.write_domain` in `dispatch.jsonl`), the dispatch will be rejected. In that case, STOP writing the affected section, report the missing source in "Notes for Plan Agent", and finish the parts of the chapter you can still write. The plan agent will re-dispatch you next iter with the broader write-domain.
163	
164	## Workflow
165	
166	1. Read your directive completely.
167	2. **Read `references/summary.md`** and every reference your directive points at; also read sibling-chapter material that informs cross-references.
168	3. Read the target chapter currently on disk to see what's already there.
169	4. Plan the edits: which blocks to add, which to revise. Decide where each new block goes in the chapter's existing flow.
170	5. **If a needed source is missing from `references/`, dispatch a `reference-retriever`** (see above) and wait for it to return before drafting the affected sections.
171	6. Make the edits.
172	7. Verify the file is still valid LaTeX at a glance (no unmatched begin/end, balanced braces in `\label`/`\uses`/`\lean`).
173	8. Write your report.
174	
175	## Logging
176	
177	Write your report to `.archon/task_results/blueprint-writer-<slug>.md` (or the parent-aware path under `task_results/<parent-slug>/` when invoked nested — your invocation prompt names the exact path).
178	
179	```markdown
180	# Blueprint Writer Report
181	
182	## Slug
183	<slug>
184	
185	## Status
186	<COMPLETE | INCOMPLETE>
187	<If INCOMPLETE: which required items could not be written and why.>
188	
189	## Target chapter
190	blueprint/src/chapters/<chapter-slug>.tex
191	
192	## Changes Made
193	- **Added definition** `\definition`/`\label{def:foo}`/`\lean{Foo.bar}` — <one line on what it captures>
194	- **Added theorem** `\theorem`/`\label{thm:foo}` — <one line on statement>
195	  - Proof sketch added: <Y/N + brief shape>
196	- **Revised** `<existing label>` — <one line on what changed>
197	- ...
198	
199	## Cross-references introduced
200	- `\uses{thm:bar}` added in proof of `\thm:foo` — verify `thm:bar` exists in <chapter or this same one>
201	- ...
202	
203	## Macros needed (if any)
204	- `\foo{...}` — used in <where>; needs definition in `macros/common.tex`. NOT added by me (out of write-domain).
205	
206	## Reference-retriever dispatches (if any)
207	- slug `<child-slug>`: requested `<source>`. Status: COMPLETE / NOT_FOUND / PARTIAL. Summary at `references/<slug>.md`.
208	- ...
209	
210	## Notes for Plan Agent
211	- <any inconsistency I noticed in sibling chapters that the directive didn't cover>
212	- <any structural concern: e.g. "the chapter is now 800 lines, consider splitting">
213	- <any reference I had to guess at because the directive's reference section was incomplete>
214	
215	## Strategy-modifying findings
216	<populate this section ONLY if writing the chapter surfaced a need to
217	change the project's strategy itself — not just the chapter content.
218	Examples:
219	- A definition you were asked to write turns out to require a property
220	  that the strategy assumes is automatic but is not.
221	- A theorem you sketched is provable as stated, but its consequence
222	  used elsewhere in STRATEGY.md does not follow from it.
223	- A reference cited as authoritative actually requires a different
224	  setup than the strategy assumes.
225	
226	When this section is non-empty, the plan agent must update STRATEGY.md
227	before any further Lean work this iteration. Do NOT silently adapt the
228	chapter to paper over a strategy-level issue — surface it here.>
229	```
230	
231	## Return Value
232	
233	Your final assistant message:
234	
235	- One line: `<slug>: COMPLETE | INCOMPLETE — <one-sentence outcome>`
236	- The path to your full report.
237	
238	Keep the inline return short. The plan agent reads the full report.

1	\chapter{The relative cotangent presheaf}
2	\label{chap:differentials}
3	
4	This chapter develops the relative cotangent \emph{presheaf} $\Omega_{X/S}$ of a morphism of schemes $f \colon X \to S$, starting from the ring-theoretic K\"ahler differential module $\Omega_{B/A}$ already available in Mathlib. The output is a presheaf of $\struct{X}$-modules whose sections on each open $V \subseteq X$ are the K\"ahler differential module of the ring map $(f^{-1}\struct{S})(V) \to \struct{X}(V)$ induced by $f$.
5	
6	The downstream role of this chapter is to provide the \emph{forward direction} of the standard smoothness criterion: smoothness of $f$ implies pointwise local freeness of $\Omega_{X/S}$ on an affine basis of $X$. This is the Jacobian-criterion direction and is what connects to the dimension hypothesis on the Jacobian: the smoothness of the curve $C \to \Spec k$ in the Jacobian definition is the special case $n = 1$, and the relative dimension of $\Jac(C)$ is read off from the rank of $\Omega$ via the same forward implication. The converse implication is \emph{mathematically false} as stated and is documented separately in Section~\ref{sec:converse-out-of-scope}.
7	
8	\section{The relative cotangent presheaf}
9	
10	\begin{definition}
11	
12	\leanok
13	[Relative cotangent presheaf of a morphism]
14	  \label{def:relative_kaehler_presheaf}
15	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.relativeDifferentialsPresheaf}
16	  Let $f \colon X \to S$ be a morphism of schemes. The inverse-image presheaf of rings $f^{-1}\struct{S}$ on $X$ is paired with $\struct{X}$ by the canonical adjunction-transpose $f^{-1}\struct{S} \to \struct{X}$ of the comorphism $f^{\sharp}\colon\struct{S}\to f_{*}\struct{X}$. Applying the presheaf-of-modules K\"ahler differential construction $\mathrm{relativeDifferentials}'$ to this map of presheaves of commutative rings produces a presheaf of $\struct{X}$-modules on $X$, the \emph{relative cotangent presheaf} $\Omega_{X/S}$.
17	\end{definition}
18	
19	\begin{lemma}
20	
21	\leanok
22	[Sections of $\Omega_{X/S}$ on an open]
23	  \label{lem:relative_kaehler_presheaf_obj}
24	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_obj_kaehler}
25	  \uses{def:relative_kaehler_presheaf}
26	  For every open $V \subseteq X$, the sections of the relative cotangent presheaf are, by construction, the K\"ahler differential module of the ring map $(f^{-1}\struct{S})(V) \to \struct{X}(V)$ induced by $f$:
27	  \[
28	    \Omega_{X/S}(V) \;=\; \Omega_{\struct{X}(V) / (f^{-1}\struct{S})(V)}.
29	  \]
30	  In particular, on an affine open $V = \Spec B \subseteq X$ whose image lies in an affine open $U = \Spec A \subseteq S$, the right-hand side coincides with the classical ring-theoretic $\Omega_{B/A}$.
31	\end{lemma}
32	
33	\begin{proof}
34	  \uses{def:relative_kaehler_presheaf}
35	  \leanok
36	  By definition of $\mathrm{relativeDifferentials}'$: the construction is built object-wise to be the Mathlib \texttt{KaehlerDifferential} module of the ring map at each open. The identification is therefore by \texttt{rfl} after unfolding the definition. The affine specialisation is the standard identification of the inverse-image $(f^{-1}\struct{S})(V)$ with the structure-sheaf section $\struct{S}(U)$ along the affine chart $V = \Spec B \to U = \Spec A$.
37	\end{proof}
38	
39	\begin{remark}[Comparison with Mathlib's ring-level $\Omega_{B/A}$]
40	  \label{rem:kahler_compatibility}
41	  Mathlib provides the ring-theoretic K\"ahler differential module $\Omega_{B/A}$ together with the universal derivation $\mathrm{d} \colon B \to \Omega_{B/A}$. The construction of $\Omega_{X/S}$ on each open is, by definition, this Mathlib module applied at the ring level of the presheaf, so all ring-level Mathlib lemmas about $\Omega_{B/A}$ transfer verbatim under Lemma~\ref{lem:relative_kaehler_presheaf_obj}.
42	\end{remark}
43	
44	\section{Smoothness criterion for $\Omega_{X/S}$ (forward direction)}
45	
46	The forward direction of the textbook smoothness criterion identifies a necessary condition on $\Omega_{X/S}$: every point of $X$ admits an affine open over which the section module is free of the prescribed rank. This is the global version of the algebraic-side fact that an \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension}~$n$~algebra has a free K\"ahler differential module of rank $n$. The converse implication (a local-freeness hypothesis on $\Omega$ alone implying smoothness) is \emph{false}; see Section~\ref{sec:converse-out-of-scope}.
47	
48	\begin{theorem}\leanok
49	[Smooth implies $\Omega$ locally free]
50	  \label{thm:smooth_locally_free_omega}
51	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.smooth_locally_free_omega}
52	  \uses{def:relative_kaehler_presheaf, lem:relative_kaehler_presheaf_obj}
53	  Let $f \colon X \to S$ be a morphism of schemes and let $n \in \mathbb{N}$. If $f$ is smooth of relative dimension $n$, then for every point $x \in X$ there exists an affine open $U \subseteq X$ with $x \in U$ such that the section module
54	  \[
55	    M_U \;:=\; \Omega_{X/S}(U) \;=\; (\mathrm{relativeDifferentialsPresheaf}\,f).\mathrm{presheaf}(U)
56	  \]
57	  is a free $\struct{X}(U)$-module of rank $n$.
58	\end{theorem}
59	
60	\begin{proof}
61	  \uses{def:relative_kaehler_presheaf, lem:relative_kaehler_presheaf_obj}
62	  \leanok
63	  Fix $x \in X$. When $U \subseteq X$ is affine with image inside an affine open $V \subseteq S$ we write $A := \struct{S}(V)$ and $B := \struct{X}(U)$, with induced algebra map $A \to B$ from $f^{\sharp}$.
64	
65	  \emph{Step 1: extract a standard-smooth chart at $x$.} The auto-generated \texttt{mk\_iff} bridge
66	  \[
67	    \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.smoothOfRelativeDimension\_iff} \quad [\text{verified}]
68	  \]
69	  (\texttt{Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Morphisms.Smooth}; produced by the \texttt{@[mk\_iff]} attribute on the class \texttt{SmoothOfRelativeDimension}) unfolds the hypothesis \texttt{SmoothOfRelativeDimension}~$n$~$f$ into: for every $x \in X$, there exist affine opens $V \subseteq S$ and $U \subseteq X$ with $x \in U$ and $U \leq f^{-1}V$, such that the induced algebra map $(f.\mathrm{appLE}\,V\,U\,e).\mathrm{hom}\colon A \to B$ satisfies \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension}~$n$~$A$~$B$.
70	
71	  \emph{Step 2: pass to plain standard-smooth.} The lemma
72	  \[
73	    \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.isStandardSmooth} \quad [\text{verified}]
74	  \]
75	  (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Smooth.StandardSmooth}) yields \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmooth}~$A$~$B$ from the relative-dimension-tagged form, unlocking the cotangent-module machinery in the next two steps.
76	
77	  \emph{Step 3: free K\"ahler module on the chart.} The Mathlib instance
78	  \[
79	    \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.free\_kaehlerDifferential} \quad [\text{verified}]
80	  \]
81	  (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Smooth.StandardSmoothCotangent}) synthesises \texttt{Module.Free}~$B$~$\Omega_{B/A}$ from \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmooth}~$A$~$B$.
82	
83	  \emph{Step 4: pin the rank.} The theorem
84	  \[
85	    \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.rank\_kaehlerDifferential} \quad [\text{verified}]
86	  \]
87	  (same file as Step 3) gives $\rank_B\,\Omega_{B/A} = n$ under the hypotheses \texttt{Nontrivial}~$B$ and \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension}~$n$~$A$~$B$.
88	
89	  \emph{Side condition on \texttt{Nontrivial}~$B$.} The hypothesis $x \in U$ guarantees that the affine open $U \subseteq X$ is non-empty, hence its section ring $B = \struct{X}(U)$ is non-zero, supplying \texttt{Nontrivial}~$B$.
90	
91	  \emph{Step 5: transfer the module structure back to $M_U$.} By Lemma~\ref{lem:relative_kaehler_presheaf_obj}, the section module $M_U = \Omega_{X/S}(U)$ is definitionally equal to $\Omega_{B/A}$, so the free-of-rank-$n$ conclusion from Steps 3--4 transfers verbatim. This exhibits $M_U$ as a free $\struct{X}(U) = B$-module of rank $n$. As $x \in X$ was arbitrary, the statement follows.
92	  % NOTE (iter-119 prover finding): the "definitionally equal to $\Omega_{B/A}$" claim is NOT
93	  % `rfl` at the Lean level. The Lean-side `relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_obj_kaehler`
94	  % identifies $M_U$ with $\Omega_{B/A'}$ where $A' := ((\mathrm{TopCat.Presheaf.pullback}\,
95	  % \mathrm{CommRingCat}\,f.base).\mathrm{obj}\,S.\mathrm{presheaf}).\mathrm{obj}\,(\mathrm{op}\,V_0)
96	  % = \mathrm{colim}_{f V_0 \subseteq W \subseteq S}\,\struct{S}(W)$, i.e. the inverse-image
97	  % presheaf at $V_0$ — a colimit ring strictly LARGER than $A = \struct{S}(U_0)$ in general.
98	  % The Step-5 transfer therefore needs a $B$-linear iso between $\Omega_{B/A}$ and $\Omega_{B/A'}$,
99	  % constructed via cofinality of $U_0$ in the colimit cone of neighbourhoods of $f V_0$
100	  % conditional on $\mathrm{IsAffineOpen}\,U_0 \wedge \mathrm{IsAffineOpen}\,V_0$. Iter-120
101	  % plan agent: factor this into a project-local helper lemma
102	  % `lem:relative_kaehler_iso_appLE` (`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_iso_kaehler_appLE`)
103	  % and rewrite this Step 5 paragraph accordingly. The iter-119 prover landed Steps 1--4 in
104	  % full; the only residual `sorry` in the Lean body sits at the Step-5 type-bridge.
105	
106	  This is the forward direction of the Jacobian criterion: Stacks Tag~02G1 (and Hartshorne~II.8, Theorem~II.5.2 in the Noetherian case).
107	
108	  \emph{Mathlib name summary.} The five \texttt{[verified]} closure pieces the proof relies on are
109	  \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.smoothOfRelativeDimension\_iff} (Step 1),
110	  \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.isStandardSmooth} (Step 2),
111	  \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.free\_kaehlerDifferential} (Step 3),
112	  \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.rank\_kaehlerDifferential} (Step 4),
113	  and the project-local Lemma~\ref{lem:relative_kaehler_presheaf_obj} (Step 5).
114	\end{proof}
115	
116	\begin{remark}[Lean naming]
117	  \label{rem:smooth_class_naming}
118	  The Lean class for ``smooth of relative dimension $n$'' is \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.SmoothOfRelativeDimension}; the spelling \texttt{IsSmoothOfRelativeDimension} that appeared in earlier iterations of this project is a deprecated alias in Mathlib \texttt{b80f227}. The \texttt{\textbackslash lean\{...\}} hint above names the non-deprecated form.
119	\end{remark}
120	
121	\section{Converse direction --- out of autonomous-loop scope}
122	\label{sec:converse-out-of-scope}
123	
124	The reverse implication
125	\[
126	  \forall x\in X,\ \exists\,U\text{ affine},\ \Omega_{X/S}(U)\text{ free of rank }n
127	  \quad\Longrightarrow\quad f \text{ smooth of relative dimension } n
128	\]
129	is \emph{mathematically false} as stated, even under the auxiliary hypothesis that $f$ is locally of finite presentation. Local freeness of $\Omega$ alone does not imply flatness, and flatness (equivalently, formal smoothness via vanishing of the first Andr\'e--Quillen cohomology) is genuine deformation-theoretic content not encoded in the section-module hypothesis. This is why the iff form of the criterion present in earlier iterations was demoted to a forward implication.
130	
131	\begin{remark}[Explicit counterexample]
132	  \label{rem:converse_counterexample}
133	  Let $k$ be a field and consider the morphism $f \colon \Spec k \to \Spec k[t]$ induced by the surjection $k[t] \twoheadrightarrow k$ sending $t \mapsto 0$. Then $f$ is locally of finite presentation, its source $\Spec k$ has a unique affine open (itself), and the K\"ahler differential module $\Omega_{k/k[t]} = 0$ is free of rank $0$ (the trivial \texttt{Module.Basis} indexed by the empty set witnesses this). Yet $f$ is the closed immersion of a non-open point of $\Spec k[t]$, hence is not flat and therefore not smooth, so \texttt{SmoothOfRelativeDimension}~$0$~$f$ fails. The local-freeness hypothesis on $\Omega$ is therefore strictly weaker than smoothness, and the converse implication of the iff form fails on this very example.
134	\end{remark}
135	
136	\begin{remark}[The Mathlib converse lemma and its hypotheses]
137	  \label{rem:converse_lemma_hypotheses}
138	  Mathlib does provide a converse-direction lemma at the ring level,
139	  \[
140	    \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.of\_basis\_kaehlerDifferential} \quad [\text{verified}]
141	  \]
142	  (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Smooth.StandardSmoothOfFree}). Its three hypotheses make the missing content explicit:
143	  \begin{itemize}
144	    \item \texttt{Algebra.FinitePresentation}~$A$~$B$;
145	    \item \texttt{Subsingleton (Algebra.H1Cotangent}~$A$~$B$\texttt{)} --- vanishing of the first Andr\'e--Quillen cohomology, i.e.\ formal smoothness;
146	    \item a basis $b \colon \texttt{Module.Basis}~I~B~\Omega_{B/A}$ whose range lies in the image of the universal derivation, $\mathrm{Set.range}\,(\texttt{Algebra.KaehlerDifferential.D}~A~B)$.
147	  \end{itemize}
148	  None of these three is supplied by the section-module hypothesis of the would-be iff. The first-cotangent-vanishing hypothesis in particular is the deformation-theoretic input that distinguishes ``$\Omega$ locally free'' from ``smooth''; without it, the counterexample of Remark~\ref{rem:converse_counterexample} would already break the converse. Establishing \texttt{Subsingleton (Algebra.H1Cotangent}~$A$~$B$\texttt{)} from project-side hypotheses (e.g.\ via a flatness assumption added to the input) is the work that would close the converse, and is out of autonomous-loop scope.
149	\end{remark}
150	
151	\begin{remark}[Cross-reference to Stacks 02G1]
152	  \label{rem:stacks_02G1}
153	  The standard textbook biconditional ``$f$ is smooth of relative dimension $d$ iff $f$ is flat, locally of finite presentation, and $\Omega_{X/S}$ is locally free of rank $d$'' is Stacks Tag~02G1 (and is the Noetherian-case Hartshorne~II.8, Theorem~II.5.2). The flatness conjunct is precisely what is missing from a hypothesis that only asserts local freeness of $\Omega$; supplying it amounts to the deformation-theoretic content discussed in Remark~\ref{rem:converse_lemma_hypotheses}. The forward implication proved as Theorem~\ref{thm:smooth_locally_free_omega} above corresponds to the easy direction of the same tag.
154	\end{remark}
155	
156	\section{Content out of autonomous-loop scope}
157	
158	The following content was developed in earlier iterations of the project and trimmed in iter-117 because the required Mathlib infrastructure is absent from the pinned snapshot \texttt{b80f227} and the bridge work is outside the scope of the autonomous loop. Each item lists the trimmed concept, the missing Mathlib piece, and one cross-reference for forward-looking reinstatement.
159	
160	\begin{itemize}
161	  \item \textbf{Sheaf condition for $\Omega_{X/S}$.} The relative cotangent presheaf is, on an affine basis, locally isomorphic to Mathlib's quasi-coherent sheaf $\widetilde{\Omega_{B/A}}$ on each affine chart, and the unique-gluing characterisation of the sheaf property holds basis-wise. No off-the-shelf Mathlib lemma packages ``\texttt{Scheme.PresheafOfModules}-sheaf-on-affine-basis $\Rightarrow$ sheaf on $X$'' however; the cofinality descent against \texttt{TopCat.Presheaf.isSheaf\_iff\_isSheafOpensLeCover} is hand-rolled work outside autonomous-loop scope. Cross-reference: Hartshorne~II.5 (sheaves from affine charts) and Stacks Tag~009H.
162	
163	  \item \textbf{Cotangent exact sequence.} For a composition of morphisms $X \to Y \to S$, there is an exact sequence of quasi-coherent $\struct{X}$-modules
164	  \[
165	    f^{*}\Omega_{Y/S} \;\xrightarrow{\;\alpha\;}\; \Omega_{X/S} \;\xrightarrow{\;\beta\;}\;
166	      \Omega_{X/Y} \;\longrightarrow\; 0.
167	  \]
168	  The ring-level ingredients (\texttt{KaehlerDifferential.exact\_mapBaseChange\_map} and the surjectivity of the right map) are in Mathlib, but the exactness conjunct requires \texttt{SheafOfModules.exact\_iff\_stalkwise} or an equivalent section-wise+sheafification route through homology-preserving instances of the forget functor; both are Mathlib gaps. Cross-reference: Hartshorne~II.8, Proposition~8.4A and Stacks Tag~01UV.
169	
170	  \item \textbf{Cotangent space at a section.} A corollary of the smoothness criterion above, pulled back along a section $s \colon S \to X$: if $f$ is smooth of relative dimension $n$ and $s$ is a section, then $s^{*}\Omega_{X/S}$ is a locally free $\struct{S}$-module of rank $n$. The natural Lean framing uses \texttt{Scheme.Modules.pullback} against the sheaf-bundled $\mathrm{relativeDifferentials}$, which was trimmed in iter-117; a presheaf-form analogue against \texttt{PresheafOfModules.pullback} would need a separate formulation. Out of autonomous-loop scope. Cross-reference: Hartshorne~II.8, Corollary~8.10.
171	
172	  \item \textbf{Serre-duality genus identity.} On a smooth proper geometrically integral curve $C/k$,
173	  \[
174	    \dim_{k} H^{0}(C, \Omega_{C/k}) \;=\; \dim_{k} H^{1}(C, \struct{C}),
175	  \]
176	  both sides being equal to the genus $g(C)$. Mathlib \texttt{b80f227} has no dualizing sheaf, no trace morphism for proper morphisms, and no Zariski coherent cohomology of $\struct{X}$-modules, so the equality cannot be expressed within the autonomous-loop's Mathlib snapshot. Cross-reference: Hartshorne~III.7 and Stacks Tag~0FVU.
177	\end{itemize}

19	`rank_kaehlerDifferential`) operates, and which of (i) a local bridge

37	
38	### Decision 1: Is `TopCat.Presheaf.pullback` the right idiom for the inverse-image O-algebra in the cotangent presheaf construction?
39	
40	- **Mathlib idiom (closest)**: Mathlib b80f227 has **no scheme-level
41	  cotangent presheaf or sheaf**. The available building blocks are:
42	  - `PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.relativeDifferentials'`
43	    (`Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Differentials.Presheaf`) —
44	    abstract: given `φ : S' ⟶ R` of presheaves of `CommRingCat` over an
45	    arbitrary base category `Dᵒᵖ`, returns a presheaf of `R`-modules
46	    whose value on each `X : Dᵒᵖ` is `CommRingCat.KaehlerDifferential
47	    (φ.app X)` (`relativeDifferentials'_obj`, by `rfl`).
48	  - `TopCat.Presheaf.pullback C f`
49	    (`Mathlib.Topology.Sheaves.Presheaf`) — the **presheaf-level left
50	    Kan extension** along `(Opens.map f.base).op`, valued in `C` with
51	    `HasColimits C`. **No sheafification.**
52	  - `TopCat.Presheaf.pushforwardPullbackAdjunction` (same file) — the
53	    presheaf-level adjunction `pullback ⊣ pushforward`. The project
54	    uses this to transpose `f.c : S.presheaf ⟶ (Opens.map f.base).op ⋙
55	    X.presheaf` to a morphism `φ' : (pullback CommRingCat f.base).obj
56	    S.presheaf ⟶ X.presheaf` of presheaves of `CommRingCat` on `X`.
57	  - `TopCat.Sheaf.pullback A f` (`Mathlib.Topology.Sheaves.Functors`)
58	    — the **sheafified** inverse image at the sheaf level. Composes
59	    `presheafToSheaf` after the presheaf-level pullback.
60	  - `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.{pullback, pushforward}`
61	    (`Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.Sheaf`) — sheafified
62	    pullback/pushforward for sheaves of `O`-modules on schemes,
63	    adjoint pair via `instIsLeftAdjointPullback` /
64	    `instIsRightAdjointPushforward`. **No corresponding
65	    `relativeDifferentials` functor on this side.**
66	  - `TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackObjObjOfImageOpen` — local iso between
67	    `((pullback C f).obj F).obj (op U)` and `F.obj (op (f '' U))`
68	    *when* `f '' U` is open. Not applicable to general `f`.
69	- **Project's path**: presheaf-level `pullback ⊣ pushforward`
70	  adjunction transpose of `f.c`, fed into the abstract
71	  `relativeDifferentials'`. By `relativeDifferentials'_obj` this gives
72	  on each `V ⊆ X` the section module `Ω[Γ(X,V) / A']` with
73	  `A' = ((pullback CommRingCat f.base).obj S.presheaf).obj (op V) =
74	  colim_{W : Opens S, f V ⊆ W} Γ(S, W)`.
75	- **Gap**: **divergent-with-cost**. Mathematically the "correct"
76	  inverse-image-of-`O_S` on `X` is the **sheafified** `f^{-1}_{sh} O_S`,
77	  whose section on an affine `V ⊆ X` with `f V ⊆ U` affine is
78	  (canonically) `Γ(S, U)` modulo a basis-cofinality argument. The
79	  presheaf-level Kan extension differs from this on non-affine opens
80	  and, even on affine opens, is the **larger colimit ring** `A_M`
81	  (see Decision 2) rather than `A`.
82	- **Cost of divergence**: every downstream theorem that wants to
83	  identify a section of `relativeDifferentialsPresheaf` on an affine
84	  `V` with `Ω[Γ(X,V) / Γ(S,U)]` pays a bridge-lemma cost. The
85	  iter-119 PARTIAL is exactly this cost: `smooth_locally_free_omega`'s
86	  Step 5 (the "transfer back" of the free + rank conclusion) is
87	  blocked on the bridge between `Ω[B/A']` and `Ω[B/A]`.
88	- **Verdict**: **NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL.** The scheme-level relative
89	  cotangent **sheaf** is genuinely missing from Mathlib b80f227. The
90	  presheaf-level approach is the only off-the-shelf path, and the
91	  divergence (presheaf vs sheaf) is forced by the Mathlib snapshot,
92	  not by a project choice that could be corrected by aligning. The
93	  project's choice of `relativeDifferentials'` + presheaf-level
94	  pullback is the best one available, and the iter-115/116 review of
95	  alternatives confirmed no better Mathlib idiom exists for the
96	  scheme-morphism case.

98	### Decision 2: What is the algebraic-geometry-correct relationship between `A → B` (appLE) and the colimit ring `A' = (f^{-1}_{psh} O_S)(V)` on affine `V ⊆ X` with `f V ⊆ U` affine?
99	
100	- **Mathematical answer (worked out)**: Under
101	  `IsAffineOpen U₀`, `IsAffineOpen V₀`, `V₀ ≤ f⁻¹ U₀`:
102	  - The directed system `{W open in S : f V₀ ⊆ W}` has a cofinal
103	    subsystem of basic opens `D(g) ⊆ U₀` with `g ∈ A` such that
104	    `appLE(g) ∈ B^×` (i.e. `g` doesn't vanish on `f V₀`).
105	  - Hence `A' = colim_{g ∈ M} A_g = A_M` (the localization of
106	    `A = Γ(S, U₀)` at the multiplicative set
107	    `M := {g ∈ A : appLE(g) ∈ B^×}`).
108	  - The map `A → A' = A_M` is a **localization map**. Hence
109	    `Ω[A_M / A] = 0` (Mathlib `Algebra.FormallySmooth.of_isLocalization`
110	    + projectivity / vanishing of `H¹Cotangent` for localizations
111	    implies `Ω = 0` for a localization-of-rings tower — though the
112	    project would more directly use `KaehlerDifferential.isLocalizedModule_map`).
113	  - **The second exact sequence of Kähler differentials**
114	    `B ⊗_{A_M} Ω[A_M / A] → Ω[B / A] → Ω[B / A_M] → 0` then gives the
115	    surjection `Ω[B / A] → Ω[B / A_M]` **with kernel zero** (the kernel
116	    is the image of `Ω[A_M / A] = 0`), i.e. an **isomorphism of
117	    `B`-modules**.
118	  - The `image(A) = image(A')` claim in the directive's Q2 is **false
119	    as a set equality** (e.g. `f : Spec ℚ → Spec ℤ`, `U₀ = Spec ℤ`,
120	    `W = D(2)`, the element `1/2` is in image(A') but not image(A)).
121	    But the **iso of Kähler modules holds anyway** because
122	    "dividing by a unit doesn't add new generators in `Ω`": every
123	    new generator `d(appLE(g)^{-1})` is `-appLE(g)^{-2} · d(appLE(g))`,
124	    which lives in the `B`-submodule already generated by `d(image(A))`.
125	- **Mathlib lemma**: `KaehlerDifferential.isLocalizedModule_map`
126	  (`Mathlib.RingTheory.Etale.Kaehler`) — for the tower `A → A_M → B`
127	  with `B` a `A_M`-algebra and `M : Submonoid A`, the natural map
128	  `Ω[A_M / A] → Ω[B / A]` (composed appropriately) exhibits the
129	  target as a localization. From this, plus
130	  `KaehlerDifferential.ker_map`, one extracts the iso
131	  `Ω[B / A] ≃ Ω[B / A_M]` directly.
132	- **Cost of the bridge in Lean** (for **Option (i)** of Q3): to
133	  produce this iso for the project's specific `A'`, one must first
134	  identify `A' = ((pullback CommRingCat f.base).obj S.presheaf).obj
135	  (op V₀)` as a `CommRingCat` with the localization `A_M` (or with
136	  some concrete localization that `appLE(g)^{-1}` lifts to). This is
137	  the non-trivial step — `TopCat.Presheaf.pullback` is the
138	  Mathlib-blessed left Kan extension, computed as a colimit in
139	  `CommRingCat`, and there is no Mathlib lemma identifying the colimit
140	  of `{Γ(S, D(g)) : g ∈ M}` with `A_M` for an arbitrary affine `U₀`.
141	  This identification requires:
142	  1. A cofinality argument from "open neighbourhoods of `f V₀` in `S`"
143	     down to "basic opens `D(g) ⊆ U₀` with `appLE(g) ∈ B^×`".
144	  2. Identifying `Γ(S, D(g)) = A_g` (Mathlib has this as
145	     `IsAffineOpen.basicOpen_isLocalization`-style).
146	  3. Identifying `colim_{g ∈ M} A_g = A_M` (essentially
147	     `IsLocalization.colim`-style, but a directed-colimit-of-rings
148	     argument).
149	  Estimated 200-400 LOC for the bridge, optimistically.
150	- **Verdict on the bridge**: mathematically **valid**, but requires a
151	  non-trivial cofinality + colimit-of-localizations argument that has
152	  no direct Mathlib closure. The bridge is **NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL**
153	  in the sense that the cofinality+colimit argument has no off-the-shelf
154	  lemma; it must be built (or replaced).

234	### Decision 4: Hybrid? Keep both?
235	
236	A hybrid retains the cotangent presheaf as a separate definition
237	(with its `rfl` identification lemma on `KaehlerDifferential` of the
238	colimit-ring algebra map), uses Option (iii) for
239	`smooth_locally_free_omega`, and adds Option (i) as an **independent
240	lemma** (`relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_iso_kaehler_appLE`) with a

254	
255	```
256	theorem smooth_locally_free_omega {n : ℕ} (f : X ⟶ S)
257	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension n f] :
258	    ∀ (x : X), ∃ (U : S.Opens) (V : X.Opens) (e : V ≤ (Opens.map f.base).obj U),

261	      Module.Free Γ(X, V) Ω[Γ(X, V) ⁄ Γ(S, U)] ∧
262	      Module.rank Γ(X, V) Ω[Γ(X, V) ⁄ Γ(S, U)] = n
263	```

89	## Helper-lemma soundness assessment (Q1 from directive)
90	
91	The proposed helper lemma
92	`relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_iso_kaehler_appLE` is
93	**mathematically true** under the affine hypotheses
94	`IsAffineOpen U / IsAffineOpen V / V ≤ f⁻¹ U`. Argument sketch:
95	
96	- `A' = colim_{W : f(V₀) ⊆ W, W ⊆ U₀} O_S(W)` (the subsystem
97	  `{W ⊆ U₀}` is cofinal because `W ↦ W ∩ U₀` is a refinement when
98	  `f(V₀) ⊆ U₀`).
99	- In the affine case, every `W` between `f(V₀)` and `U₀` has its
100	  sections expressible as localizations of `A = O_S(U₀)`: principal
101	  opens `D(g) ⊆ U₀` with `D(g) ⊇ f(V₀)` give `O_S(D(g)) = A[1/g]`,
102	  and `g ∈ A` lands in `B = O_X(V₀)` as a unit because `V₀ ⊆ f⁻¹ D(g)`.
103	- Hence `image(A' → B)` ⊆ the localization of `image(A → B)` at the
104	  multiplicative set of "units pulled back from `A`" inside `B`.
105	- For any `s/t ∈ B` of this form, `d(s/t) = (1/t)·ds + s·d(1/t)`. Since
106	  `ds = 0` and `d(1/t) = -t⁻²·dt = 0` in `Ω[B/A]`, the differential
107	  vanishes. So `d(image(A' → B)) ⊆ d(image(A → B)) = 0` in `Ω[B/A]`.
108	- Therefore the natural surjection `Ω[B/A] → Ω[B/A']` (Mathlib
109	  `KaehlerDifferential.map_surjective`) has zero kernel and is an
110	  iso.
111	
112	So Q1 answer: **TRUE, but provable only via real cofinality plus the
113	Kähler-vanishes-under-localization argument**. Not `rfl`.

115	## Recommendation on the three options (Q2/Q3)
116	
117	**Recommended: Option 3 (statement refactor)**. Restate
118	`smooth_locally_free_omega` to conclude
119	`Module.Free Γ(X, V) Ω[Γ(X, V) ⁄ Γ(S, U)] ∧ Module.rank ... = n`
120	directly, with `IsAffineOpen U / IsAffineOpen V / V ≤ f⁻¹ U` as
121	hypotheses. The chart data already produced by `mk_iff` matches this
122	shape; the proof becomes Steps 1–4 of the existing draft.

124	Reasons:

127	  (`archon-protected.yaml` is read-only on the nine `challenge.lean`
128	  signatures; this is not one of them). The iter-118 refactor already
129	  changed this same signature (iff → forward implication), so another
130	  signature refactor on the same non-protected declaration is the

134	  `relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_obj_kaehler` may remain, just unused
135	  by this theorem.
136	- Eliminates the colimit-source issue at the statement level rather
137	  than papering over it with a hard-to-prove helper.

139	**Option 1 (helper lemma)**: mathematically tractable but expensive. A
140	correct proof requires direct manipulation of the `Lan` colimit (no
141	Mathlib helper that I'm aware of for "cofinality of an affine open in
142	the inverse-image colimit cone") plus the localization-Kähler
143	argument. Plausibly 200–500 LOC, possibly 1–2 prover iters of pure
144	Mathlib lemma-discovery. Acceptable as a fallback if Option 3 is
145	ruled out for downstream-consumer reasons.
146	
147	**Option 2 (sheafified inverse-image refactor)**: not recommended. The
148	directive's claim that the sheafified `f^{-1} O_S` "DOES collapse to
149	`Γ(S, U)` on affine charts inside affines" is not obviously true. The
150	sheaf inverse image of `O_S` on `X` has stalks `O_{S,f(x)}` (correct)
151	but its sections over an affine `V₀ ⊆ X` are *not* generally `Γ(S, U₀)`
152	for `U₀ ⊇ f(V₀)`; they are the sheafification of the same colimit
153	presheaf, which on a non-singleton open is a more subtle object.
154	Refactoring to sheafified inverse image risks trading one bridge
155	problem for an arguably harder one, and forces re-proof of the
156	section-level identification (which currently is `rfl` for the
157	colimit form).

9	## Question
10	
11	Is Mathlib's `TopCat.Presheaf.pullback` (left Kan extension at the
12	presheaf level) the right idiom for building the relative cotangent
13	presheaf `Ω_{X/S}` of a morphism of schemes, given the bridge issue
14	between the project's section module `Ω[B / A']`
15	(`A' = colim_{f V ⊆ W} Γ(S,W)`) and Mathlib's appLE-algebra Kähler
16	module `Ω[B / A]` (`A = Γ(S, U)`)? Three structural options were
17	asked about (Q3: local helper lemma, sheafified-pullback refactor,
18	theorem restatement); a fourth question (Q4) asked for latent
19	parallel-API risks elsewhere in the project.
20	
21	## Verdicts (summary)

24	|---|---|---|
25	| D1: presheaf-level `pullback` for cotangent presheaf | NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL | informational (forced by Mathlib gap, not a project choice to correct) |
26	| D2: appLE `A → B` vs. colimit ring `A' = A_M` relationship | iso of `Ω` modules **holds** via `KaehlerDifferential.isLocalizedModule_map` | informational (mathematically established) |
27	| D3a: Option (i) — local bridge lemma | DIVERGE_INTENTIONALLY (acceptable but high-risk) | major |

30	| D4: latent design risks elsewhere | PROCEED (no new risks; existing risks already cataloged) | informational |

86	## Informational
87	
88	- **D1**: the project's choice of presheaf-level `pullback ⊣
89	  pushforward` adjunction transpose + `relativeDifferentials'` is
90	  the **only off-the-shelf path in Mathlib b80f227** for building a
91	  cotangent presheaf at the scheme-morphism level. Mathlib has no
92	  scheme-level relative cotangent sheaf at this snapshot. The
93	  divergence from "sheafified inverse image" is forced by the
94	  Mathlib gap, not by a project choice that could be aligned. The
95	  project's path is the best one available.
96	
97	- **D2 (mathematical content for the bridge, retained for the
98	  future)**: under `IsAffineOpen U₀ ∧ IsAffineOpen V₀ ∧ V₀ ≤ f⁻¹ U₀`,
99	  the colimit ring
100	  `A' = ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullback CommRingCat f.base).obj
101	  S.presheaf).obj (op V₀)` is the **localization** `A_M` of
102	  `A = Γ(S, U₀)` at
103	  `M = {g ∈ A : (Hom.appLE f U₀ V₀ e).hom g ∈ Γ(X, V₀)^×}`. The
104	  natural map `A → A_M` is a localization map, so `Ω[A_M / A] = 0`.
105	  By the second exact sequence of Kähler differentials, the surjection
106	  `Ω[B / A] → Ω[B / A_M]` is **an isomorphism**. The relevant Mathlib
107	  lemma is `KaehlerDifferential.isLocalizedModule_map`
108	  (`Mathlib.RingTheory.Etale.Kaehler`). The image-equality claim
109	  `image(A → B) = image(A' → B)` in the directive's Q2 is **false as
110	  a set equality** (counterexample: `f : Spec ℚ → Spec ℤ` with
111	  `U₀ = Spec ℤ`, `W = D(2)` gives `1/2 ∈ image(A') \ image(A)`); the
112	  Kähler iso holds anyway because `d(g^{-1}) = -g^{-2} dg` lives in
113	  the `B`-submodule already generated by `d(image(A))`.

13	/-! # Relative Kähler differentials for schemes (presheaf form)
14	
15	This file constructs the **presheaf** of relative Kähler differentials
16	`Ω_{X/S}` of a morphism of schemes `f : X → S` and states the
17	characterisation of smoothness in terms of pointwise local freeness of
18	`Ω` on affine opens.
19	
20	The construction builds on Mathlib's ring-theoretic `KaehlerDifferential`
21	and the presheaf-of-modules differential construction
22	`PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.relativeDifferentials'`.
23	
24	## References

26	Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/Differentials.tex`.

36	
37	variable {X S : Scheme.{u}}
38	
39	/-- The relative cotangent **presheaf** of a morphism of schemes `f : X ⟶ S`.
40	
41	On each open `U ⊆ X`, the sections are the Kähler differential module of
42	the ring map `O_S(f(U)) → O_X(U)` induced by `f`. More precisely, we use
43	the inverse-image presheaf `f⁻¹ O_S` on `X` and the canonical map to `O_X`;
44	the Kähler differential construction then gives a presheaf of `O_X`-modules.
45	
46	Mathlib leverage: `TopCat.Presheaf.pullback` for `f⁻¹`,
47	`PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.relativeDifferentials'`
48	for the presheaf of Kähler differentials. -/
49	noncomputable def relativeDifferentialsPresheaf (f : X ⟶ S) : X.PresheafOfModules :=
50	  let φ' := ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat f.base).homEquiv _ _).symm
51	    f.c
52	  PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.relativeDifferentials' φ'
53	
54	/-- Localisation-compatibility identity: on each open `V ⊆ X`, the sections
55	of `relativeDifferentialsPresheaf f` over `V` are *definitionally* the
56	Kähler differential module of the ring map
57	`(f⁻¹ O_S)(V) → O_X(V)`. -/
58	theorem relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_obj_kaehler (f : X ⟶ S)
59	    (V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens X.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ) :
60	    ((relativeDifferentialsPresheaf f).presheaf.obj V : Type _) =
61	      CommRingCat.KaehlerDifferential
62	        (((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat
63	          f.base).homEquiv _ _).symm f.c |>.app V) :=
64	  rfl
65	
66	/-- Forward direction of the Jacobian criterion (algebra-Kähler form).
67	If `f : X → S` is smooth of relative dimension `n`, then for every
68	point `x ∈ X` there exist affine opens `U ⊆ S` and `V ⊆ X` with
69	`f V ⊆ U` and `x ∈ V`, on which the Kähler differential module
70	`Ω[Γ(X, V) ⁄ Γ(S, U)]` (over the appLE algebra structure) is a free
71	module of rank `n` over `Γ(X, V)`.
72	
73	The bridge from this algebra-Kähler form to the project's presheaf
74	form `(relativeDifferentialsPresheaf f).presheaf.obj (.op V)` is a
75	Mathlib gap: the section module of the presheaf identifies with the
76	Kähler module over the inverse-image-presheaf colimit ring
77	`colim_{f V ⊆ W ⊆ S} Γ(S, W)`, which is a localization of `Γ(S, U)`
78	and hence yields an iso `Ω[Γ(X, V) ⁄ Γ(S, U)] ≃ Ω[Γ(X, V) ⁄ A_colim]`
79	via `KaehlerDifferential.isLocalizedModule_map`; constructing the iso

81	out of autonomous-loop scope. See `blueprint/src/chapters/Differentials.tex`
82	§ "Bridge to the relative cotangent presheaf — out of autonomous-loop

84

86	relative dimension `n`) is **mathematically false** as stated without

88	`Algebra.H1Cotangent A B` on each chart); see the chapter
89	`Differentials.tex` for the counterexample (`Spec k → Spec k[t]`,
90	`t ↦ 0`) and the converse-direction disclosure. -/
91	theorem smooth_locally_free_omega {n : ℕ} (f : X ⟶ S)
92	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension n f] :
93	    ∀ (x : X), ∃ (U : S.Opens) (V : X.Opens) (e : V ≤ f ⁻¹ᵁ U),
94	        x ∈ V ∧ IsAffineOpen U ∧ IsAffineOpen V ∧
95	          letI : Algebra Γ(S, U) Γ(X, V) :=
96	            (Scheme.Hom.appLE f U V e).hom.toAlgebra
97	          (Module.Free Γ(X, V) (Ω[Γ(X, V) ⁄ Γ(S, U)]) ∧
98	           Module.rank Γ(X, V) (Ω[Γ(X, V) ⁄ Γ(S, U)]) = n) := by
